subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Starburst Galaxies
description: A quick win: understand Starburst Galaxies.

hook.text: Starburst galaxies form stars at 100 times the normal rate. They blaze with the light of trillions of young stars.

buildup.text: Most starbursts are triggered by galaxy mergers. The collision compresses gas, sparking intense waves of star formation.

discovery.text: M82, the Cigar Galaxy, is the nearest starburst galaxy. Its center glows with infrared light from millions of new stars.

twist.text: Starbursts can't last forever. They burn through gas in just a few hundred million years, then go quiet.

climax.text: Superwinds from the burst blow gas out of the galaxy entirely, shutting down future star formation.

punchline.text: A hundred times the normal rate. Brief, brilliant, and self-destructive.

quiz.question: What typically triggers a starburst in a galaxy?
options: A galaxy merger that compresses gas and sparks intense star formation
A supermassive black hole that heats all the gas at once
Dark energy accelerating the rotation of the galaxy
